goals.g1: Determine whether SchemaCore/compile_core could precompute, statically from graph2, which nodes are bijectively mapped (clean 1:1 inherit, no fan-in/fan-out), have an unchanged edge spectrum (same internal+crossing (type,direction) categories before/after), and are transitively connected to other such nodes -- to replace reverse_footprint_lightweight's coarse over-approximation with a precise, schema-level unchanged/changed distinction.

prep_note: Sven's idea, this session. Prepared before checking existing code.

prior_candidates.statement: Implemented in basic_machinery/reverse_compound.py: build_reversed_operation (wraps build_reversed_graph2+compile_schema, cached), collect_reversed_matches (phase 1, blind match_all per rule, flags per-rule multiplicity), resolve_reverse_compound (phase 2, OV_forward vs OV_reverse structural comparison via detect_overlaps reused unmodified twice -- no ordering, no enumeration, per this session's correction). Verified end-to-end against a single-rule synthetic case using an explicit-category rule. NOT verified: any genuine multi-rule compound firing (no test built); footprint_is_consistent is still not wired to a real apply_compound aggregate; the full isomorphism-to-retained-previous-layer check is not implemented at all -- resolve_reverse_compound only gets as far as the OV comparison. A real, uncorrected defect was found and left uncorrected (root_cause_and_rule_misapplied): reversed rules built from a forward rule using ph_all_four (blanket, variable-presence preservation) will incorrectly find zero matches against real firings where fewer than all 4 declared categories are actually present, because the exact-degree matcher cannot express 'however many of these are present'. This affects any real rule using ph_all_four (at least spine_finalise_v1.py's out_rspine, confirmed earlier this session) and is carried forward, not fixed.

derived_from.rule: state plainly what is verified working, what is unverified, and what is a known, real, uncorrected defect -- not overclaiming completeness

goals: Implement the corrected reversibility check design (per ers_lf6_repair.json and ers_compose_reversed_compound.json's surviving structure): collect_reversed_matches(matches, result_graph) -- per-rule blind match_all of each reversed rule against the result, flagging per-rule multiplicity; resolve_reverse_compound -- compare OV_forward (detect_overlaps on the known forward matches/base graph) against OV_reverse (detect_overlaps on the reverse matches/result graph), no enumeration, no ordering; reverse_fire -- thin orchestrator wiring these to footprint_is_consistent (existing) and the isomorphism check.

prior_candidates.statement: _read_boundary_externals(edits, binding, graph) (schema.py) ALREADY implements the correct AND for the forward direction: for each output node's DECLARED boundary_grab category, it reads LIVE (per this specific binding, before teardown) whether a real edge actually exists via graph.edges_from/edges_to, returning ONLY the confirmed-present (out_node -> [(type,direction,outside_node)]) categories -- declared-and-existing, not merely declared.
goals: Fix the ph_all_four zero-match bug using Sven's physical-switch idea: when a crossing's existence AND declaration both hold (confirmed per the actual firing via _read_boundary_externals), directly exchange the placeholder mapping between the input and mapped-output sides, per binding -- rather than requiring a fixed, static, blanket set of categories (all 4 of ph_all_four) that may not all be present in any given real firing.

prior_candidates.statement: _read_boundary_externals(edits, binding, graph) (schema.py) ALREADY implements the correct AND for the forward direction: for each output node's DECLARED boundary_grab category, it reads LIVE (per this specific binding, before teardown) whether a real edge actually exists via graph.edges_from/edges_to, returning ONLY the confirmed-present (out_node -> [(type,direction,outside_node)]) categories -- declared-and-existing, not merely declared.
goals: Verify and record the physical-switch fix's actual test result: does re-running the exact ph_all_four case that previously found zero matches now succeed?

prior_candidates.statement: ADOPTED: cand_lightweight_aggregate, scoped to the no-overlap case (matches this session's tested coverage). S_forward comes directly from a REAL forward firing via apply_compound's own return value (result['born']|result['changed']) -- no new code needed there. S_reverse_implied is computed by a new lightweight function: for the reversed matches (no overlaps case), born = reversed schema's core.born positions mapped through the reversed output_map, changed = incident-set comparison before/after for survived positions -- mirroring apply_compound's aggregation without the full LayeredGraph/registry integration (cand_reuse_apply_compound_dryrun failed its falsifier: no PerspectiveGraph-to-LayeredGraph adapter exists, real unbuilt integration work). SCOPE LIMIT, carried: this lightweight version does not incorporate resolve_compound's redirect logic, so it is only confirmed adequate for the no-overlap case already tested -- a genuine compound/overlapping reverse firing would need more.

derived_from.rule: adopt the candidate that survived its falsifier (self-contained, no new untested integration surface) over the one that failed (requires building an adapter that doesn't exist), while honestly scoping the adopted candidate's own limit

goals: Continue the reversibility checker implementation: wire footprint_is_consistent to real apply_compound aggregates (S_forward/S_reverse_implied), build a reverse_fire orchestrator tying collect_reversed_matches + resolve_reverse_compound + footprint_is_consistent together, and determine what the isomorphism-to-retained-previous-layer check actually requires to implement against real graphs.

S_forward comes directly from a REAL forward firing via apply_compound's own return value (result['born']|result['changed']) -- no new code needed there. goals: Record the reverse_fire orchestrator implementation and its first end-to-end test result, including the honest scope limits (coarse footprint approximation, no-overlap-only, no isomorphism check, stand-in S_forward since no apply_compound/LayeredGraph adapter exists).

sven_precompute_idea.statement: Sven (direct, this session): the schema already precomputes various things from graph2 -- did I consider having it also precompute which nodes are bijectively mapped, whether their edge spectrum is unchanged, and whether they're connected to other such nodes, to distinguish unchanged from changed nodes properly?

code_inherit_already_bijective.statement: compile_core's inherit assignment (schema.py) is already documented and constructed as INJECTIVE: 'each input may be inherited by AT MOST ONE output (a real node cannot become two outputs)'. core.inherit's domain (its keys, the out_nodes with an inherit entry) IS already exactly the set of bijectively-mapped nodes -- this part of Sven's idea requires NO new computation, it already exists.

no_edge_spectrum_comparison_exists.statement: Nothing in SchemaCore currently compares the INPUT side's declared category spectrum (internal edges among input_nodes touching in_node, plus expected_crossing from derive_match_view) against the OUTPUT side's declared spectrum (core.internal_edges touching out_node, plus core.boundary_grab) for a given inherit pair. This comparison does not exist and would need building.

connectivity_propagation_matches_anchor_.statement: Propagating 'unchanged' status to nodes connected to other confirmed-unchanged nodes is exactly the ANCHOR mechanism's original intent (ers_reversibility_anchor: unchanged nodes as known-identity anchors, growing correspondence outward) -- but built on a PRECISE, schema-derivable foundation (bijective + matching declared spectrum) instead of the coarse over-approximation in reverse_footprint_lightweight (which currently treats ALL touched positions as equally 'changed', explicitly flagged as capable of false mismatches).

combined_design.statement: Two-part precomputation, correctly scoped: (1) STATIC, schema-level, computed once per rule (new function compute_unchanged_candidates(graph2)): a node is a CANDIDATE-unchanged node iff (a) bijectively mapped (out_node in core.inherit -- already guaranteed injective, no new work) AND (b) its declared spectrum matches: internal-edges-touching-in_node union expected_crossing-categories-touching-in_node (input side) EQUALS internal-edges-touching-out_node union boundary_grab-categories-touching-out_node (output side). (2) PER-FIRING, using the already-built _read_boundary_externals: a candidate node is CONFIRMED-unchanged for a specific firing iff its confirmed crossings (exists AND declared) exactly match its declared crossings (i.e. nothing was merely-declared-but-absent for this firing, closing the ph_all_four-style gap). (3) CONNECTIVITY: confirmed-unchanged nodes connected to other confirmed-unchanged nodes extend the anchor region transitively -- flood-fill outward from any confirmed-unchanged node through edges to other confirmed-unchanged nodes.

derived_from.rule: combine the free static part (bijective, already computed) with a new static spectrum-match narrowing, and require per-firing confirmation to close the declared-vs-actual gap already found this session

chk_falsifier_declared_spectrum_sufficie.method: does a DECLARED spectrum match (input-side declared categories == output-side declared categories) actually guarantee a node is unchanged, or does the earlier ph_all_four finding (declared != confirmed present) undermine this by itself?
chk_falsifier_declared_spectrum_sufficie.result: Declared-spectrum matching is NECESSARY but NOT SUFFICIENT by itself -- exactly the same gap found earlier (ph_all_four blanket declarations don't guarantee actual presence). A node could have matching DECLARED spectra on both sides while its ACTUAL crossings still differ per firing. The precomputation must be combined with the per-firing CONFIRMED crossings (_read_boundary_externals, already built for the physical-switch fix) to get a real per-firing 'unchanged' verdict -- the static schema-level part narrows WHICH nodes are even CANDIDATES for unchanged (bijective + declared-spectrum-match), and the per-firing confirmed-crossing check settles it definitively for that specific firing.

side_findings.finding: declared-spectrum match alone is not sufficient to prove a node unchanged; must combine with per-firing confirmed crossings

chk_falsifier_combined.method: does requiring in_node's internal edges to compare against out_node's internal edges even make sense structurally -- these are nodes in DIFFERENT roles (input_nodes set vs output_nodes set) of the same graph2, are their 'internal_edges' comparable at all?
chk_falsifier_combined.result: Comparable with care: core.internal_edges is specifically (out_src,out_tgt,EdgeType) among OUTPUT nodes only; the INPUT side's analogous internal structure is not precomputed anywhere in SchemaCore currently -- it would need a symmetric 'input_internal_edges' computation (edges among input_nodes, mirroring how internal_edges is computed for output_nodes). This is real, additional new code, not simply reading an existing field the way core.internal_edges/boundary_grab already are.

side_findings.finding: input-side internal edges are not currently precomputed in SchemaCore and would need a new, symmetric computation added

chk_relevance_combined.method: connect combined_design's evidence to this session's stated goal
chk_relevance_combined.result: Goal: determine whether schema could precompute bijective/spectrum-unchanged/connected nodes to replace the coarse approximation. combined_design directly specifies this, scoped correctly with the per-firing confirmation step the earlier ph_all_four finding requires.

committed.statement: Two-part precomputation, correctly scoped: (1) STATIC, schema-level, computed once per rule (new function compute_unchanged_candidates(graph2)): a node is a CANDIDATE-unchanged node iff (a) bijectively mapped (out_node in core.inherit -- already guaranteed injective, no new work) AND (b) its declared spectrum matches: internal-edges-touching-in_node union expected_crossing-categories-touching-in_node (input side) EQUALS internal-edges-touching-out_node union boundary_grab-categories-touching-out_node (output side). (2) PER-FIRING, using the already-built _read_boundary_externals: a candidate node is CONFIRMED-unchanged for a specific firing iff its confirmed crossings (exists AND declared) exactly match its declared crossings (i.e. nothing was merely-declared-but-absent for this firing, closing the ph_all_four-style gap). (3) CONNECTIVITY: confirmed-unchanged nodes connected to other confirmed-unchanged nodes extend the anchor region transitively -- flood-fill outward from any confirmed-unchanged node through edges to other confirmed-unchanged nodes.
